name: Grain
one_liner: AI meeting-notetaker that records, transcribes, and summarizes calls, with a REST API exposing recordings, transcripts, notes, action items, deals, and coaching data.
access_notes: Users self-generate Personal Access Tokens (PAT) or Workspace Access Tokens (WAT) in-app at grain.com/app/settings/integrations?tab=api, no approval or payment needed. OAuth2 (auth code + PKCE) is available for third-party integrations but requires manual client registration. Most API/MCP data (meetings, transcripts, notes, contacts) works on all plans including free; Deals and Sales Coaching/Scorecard endpoints are restricted to Business/Enterprise plans.
api_notes: REST/JSON API (header Public-Api-Version: 2025-10-31, Bearer token). Resources: recordings (list, get, update title, download, upload, transcripts in JSON/TXT/VTT/SRT, tags, sharing), highlights/clips, action items, AI summaries, calendar metadata, HubSpot integration data, users, teams, meeting types, and webhook (hooks) management. Rate limit 300 req/min (429 + Retry-After). Roughly 20-40 operations.
mcp_notes: Grain ships an official MCP server (announced 06-18-2025, docs at developers.grain.com/mcp). Uses OAuth; ~15-22 tools (list/fetch meetings, transcripts, notes, search meetings/companies/persons, workspace users, plus Business/Enterprise-only deals and coaching tools). Setup as native Claude Integration or via mcp-remote in Cursor/Windsurf.
main_blocker: None for core meeting/transcript/notes data - self-serve tokens on any plan. Deals and sales-coaching endpoints require a Business/Enterprise plan.

evidence.claim: Official developer docs: REST API with PAT, WAT, and OAuth2 (PKCE) auth; header Public-Api-Version; recordings, transcripts, hooks, etc.
Official Grain MCP server docs listing tools and plan-tiered access (free/starter vs Business/Enterprise).
Grain release note announcing its official MCP server (06-18-2025).

open_questions: Whether a fully free Grain account (vs a paid trial) can generate a working PAT/WAT, and exactly how many distinct REST operations exist (breadth estimated as MODERATE).
